commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a five-model static OpenCode Zen catalog, live model discovery, dynamic resolution, family-specific transports, shared credential lookup, endpoint normalization, manifest metadata, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +471, Tests +272. Total +743 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level with high confidence: current main and v2026.6.6 select a Zen default model but the bundled plugin has no catalog or dynamic resolver, matching the linked `model_not_found` path. This read-only review did not run a real credentialed failing request.

target.title: fix(opencode): restore Zen model catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Restore a plugin-owned OpenCode Zen model catalog for the bundled `opencode` provider.
- Wire Zen catalog runtime hooks: static curated seed, live `/zen/v1/models` discovery, dynamic model resolution, model-catalog augmentation, and base URL normalization.
- Restore family-specific Zen transports so Claude/Minimax rows use Anthropic Messages on `/zen`, GPT rows use OpenAI Responses on `/zen/v1`, Gemini rows use the Google adapter, and fallback rows stay OpenAI-compatible.
- Add focused coverage for static Claude seed rows, live-discovered-only rows, shared OpenCode auth handling, runtime discovery metadata, manifest transport metadata, and stale Zen base URL canonicalization.

Fixes #92479

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `opencode` no longer ships without catalog or dynamic-resolution hooks. A configured Zen model such as `opencode/claude-opus-4-8` resolves from the bundled curated seed, live discovery can synthesize additional Zen rows from `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models`, and Zen model rows keep their provider-family transport contract instead of being collapsed to `openai-completions`.

**Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `1ee1032f`. Local proof used a dummy OpenCode env key for catalog fallback visibility and mocked live discovery for live-only row synthesis; no real OpenCode credentials or live model requests were used.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . test extensions/opencode/index.test.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec oxlint extensions/opencode/index.ts extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts extensions/opencode/index.test.ts
OPENCODE_API_KEY=dummy corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . openclaw models list --provider opencode --json
OPENCODE_API_KEY=dummy corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . openclaw models list --provider opencode
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . plugins:inventory:check
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the real OpenClaw CLI shows the bundled Zen provider returning the curated static rows after live discovery timed out and fell back locally. Focused runtime tests also assert the transport metadata that the CLI table does not display: `claude-opus-4-8`/static manifest rows use `anthropic-messages` with `https://opencode.ai/zen`, while live-only `gpt-5.5` uses `openai-responses` with `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1`.

```text
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs models list --provider opencode --json
[fetch-timeout] fetch timeout after 5000ms (elapsed 5001ms) operation=fetchWithSsrFGuard url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models
{
  "count": 5,
  "models": [
    { "key": "opencode/claude-fable-5", "name": "Claude Fable 5", "input": "text+image", "contextWindow": 200000, "local": false, "available": true, "tags": [], "missing": false },
    { "key": "opencode/claude-opus-4-6", "name": "Claude Opus 4.6", "input": "text+image", "contextWindow": 200000, "local": false, "available": true, "tags": [], "missing": false },
    { "key": "opencode/claude-opus-4-7", "name": "Claude Opus 4.7", "input": "text+image", "contextWindow": 200000, "local": false, "available": true, "tags": [], "missing": false },
    { "key": "opencode/claude-opus-4-8", "name": "Claude Opus 4.8", "input": "text+image", "contextWindow": 200000, "local": false, "available": true, "tags": [], "missing": false },
    { "key": "opencode/claude-sonnet-4-6", "name": "Claude Sonnet 4.6", "input": "text+image", "contextWindow": 200000, "local": false, "available": true, "tags": [], "missing": false }
  ]
}

$ node scripts/run-node.mjs models list --provider opencode
[fetch-timeout] fetch timeout after 5000ms (elapsed 5001ms) operation=fetchWithSsrFGuard url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models
Model                                      Input      Ctx         Local Auth  Tags
opencode/claude-fable-5                    text+image 195k        no    yes
opencode/claude-opus-4-6                   text+image 195k        no    yes
opencode/claude-opus-4-7                   text+image 195k        no    yes
opencode/claude-opus-4-8                   text+image 195k        no    yes
opencode/claude-sonnet-4-6                 text+image 195k        no    yes

$ corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . test extensions/opencode/index.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  10 passed (10)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 25.44s

$ corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec oxlint extensions/opencode/index.ts extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts extensions/opencode/index.test.ts
# no output

$ corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . plugins:inventory:check
$ node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check
```

**Observed result after fix:** `openclaw models list --provider opencode` shows five `opencode/*` Zen model rows from the curated fallback catalog. Focused tests verify static seed resolution, cached live discovery, live-only row synthesis, shared OpenCode auth handling, runtime discovery metadata, manifest transport metadata, Claude Anthropic routing, GPT Responses routing, and stale Zen base URL canonicalization.

**What was not tested:** Real OpenCode credentials, live `/zen/v1/models` success against the service, and live model completions were not run. Full `pnpm build` was attempted locally before this PR body refresh, but the `tsdown` phase was killed with SIGTERM after a long no-output period without a TypeScript diagnostic, so it is not claimed as passing here.
